title: Arabithittu Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Arabithittu Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Arabithittu Wildlife Sanctuary is located in the Mysore district of Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Arabithittu Wildlife Sanctuary is located in the Mysore district of Karnataka, India. It was declared as a wildlife sanctuary on 30 April 1985. It was originally called the Arabithittu Game Reserve. The sanctuary has a large area of eucalyptus and sandalwood plantations.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Arabithittu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Arabithittu Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Arabithittu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (29 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (25 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Arabithittu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Arabithittu Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Arabithittu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Arabithittu Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Arabithittu Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hosaramanahalli, Bilikere, Mysore, St. Anthony's Basilica, Dornahalli.
